fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to new hampshire, respiratory therapist compact new hampshire, new hampshire respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Hampshire Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in New Hampshire.

steps.title: Create an OPLC Account and Start Application
steps.description: Register for an NH.gov Login and access the OPLC Online Licensing Portal to initiate a new Respiratory Care Practitioner license application.

steps.title: Complete Application & Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form and submit the non-refundable application fee ($110) through the OPLC Online Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from an accredited respiratory care program, verification of NBRC RRT credential, and any other specified supporting documents (e.g., Letters of Good Standing from other states if applicable) to be sent directly to the OPLC.

steps.title: Pass the Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Complete and pass the New Hampshire Respiratory Care Jurisprudence Examination, which may be available online or require a separate submission.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Record Check (CRC)
steps.description: Submit to a fingerprint-based criminal record check as required by the board. This involves specific forms and a separate fee ($48.25) paid directly to the NH State Police.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in New Hampshire. To work in New Hampshire, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in New Hampshire
hero.dek: Apply for a New Hampshire Respiratory Care Practitioner license through the OPLC's Online Licensing Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to the OPLC Online Licensing Portal. 2. Click 'Go to the Online Licensing Portal'. 3. If you have an existing NH.gov Login, log in. 4. If not, click 'Create an Account' to register for an NH.gov Login. 5. Once logged in, you can select and start a new application for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license.
